g.data-narration: Welcome to the Stack Data Structure lecture. Today we'll explore this fundamental LIFO-based data structure in detail.
A stack is a linear data structure following the Last In, First Out principle. Think of a stack of plates - add or remove only from the top.

text.tspan: A stack is a linear data structure that follows the
principle. The element inserted last is removed first.

g.data-narration: The Stack ADT defines five key operations: push to add, pop to remove, peek to view, isEmpty and isFull to check status.
Let's visualize LIFO: push 10, 20, 30. Stack grows upward. Pop returns 30 first, then 20, then 10 - reversing the push order.
g.text: Note: top always points to the most recently added element. When empty, top = -1

g.data-narration: The array-based implementation uses a fixed-size array and an integer top variable to track the index of the topmost element.
The push operation increments top, then stores the value at that index. It must check for overflow before inserting.
The pop operation retrieves and removes the top element, then decrements top. It must check for underflow before popping.
Peek returns the top element without removing it. isEmpty checks if top equals -1, isFull checks if top equals MAX-1.
Stack Overflow occurs when pushing to a full stack (top == MAX-1). Stack Underflow occurs when popping from an empty stack (top == -1).
g.text: When: top == MAX - 1 | Action: Cannot push

g.data-narration: All stack operations have O(1) time complexity because they only access or modify the top element - no iteration needed.
g.text: All operations are constant time because they only access/modify the top element!

g.data-narration: Stacks have many applications: function call management, expression evaluation, parenthesis matching, backtracking algorithms, and string reversal.
A key application is checking if brackets are balanced. Push opening brackets, pop and match when closing brackets appear.
In summary, stacks follow LIFO with O(1) operations. Key points: top=-1 when empty, overflow/underflow conditions, and struct-based implementation.
g.text: Principle: LIFO (Last In, First Out) | Operations: push, pop, peek, isEmpty, isFull
Implementation: Array + top index | Empty: top = -1 | Full: top = MAX-1
Time Complexity: O(1) for all core operations |
